name: Bouranee Baunjaun ( Eggplant with Yoghurt Sauce )
recipeInstructions:
1. Remove stems from eggplants and do not peel the skin. Cut into slices that are 1 cm (1/2-inch) thick.
2. Place the sliced eggplant on a tray and sprinkle generously with salt. Let sit for 30 minutes, then pat dry thoroughly with paper towels.
3. Heat enough oil in a deep frying pan (with lid) to cover the bottom well. Fry eggplant slices until lightly browned on both sides but not fully cooked. Transfer to a plate when done. Add more oil if needed for additional slices.
4. Return some of the used oil to the pan, then add sliced onions and fry gently until translucent. Remove and set aside.
5. Place another layer of eggplant in the pan. Arrange with onion slices, green pepper rings, and tomato pieces. Sprinkle a little salt and chili pepper between layers. Pour any remaining oil from the eggplant and onions into the pan, then add water. Cover and simmer gently for 10-15 minutes until the eggplant is tender.
6. In a separate bowl, combine chakah ingredients. Spread half of the yogurt sauce in the base of the serving dish. Arrange vegetables on top, being careful to keep slices intact. Leave some juices in the pan.
7. Finish by adding the remainder of the yogurt sauce over the vegetables and drizzle with vegetable juices. Serve with Kabaub and Lawash (flat bread).
